entries.expected_behavior: seasonJoin() clears idle_since_tick to NULL, matching the idleAck() cleanup pattern, so no stale idle-hold value persists after a rejoin.
entries.observed_behavior: seasonJoin() set activity_state='Active' and idle_modal_active=0 but left idle_since_tick stale from a prior idle transition. The stale value has no functional impact (resolveEconomicPresenceState short-circuits on activity_state='Active'), but is inconsistent with idleAck.
entries.linked_fix: includes/actions.php — seasonJoin() now sets idle_since_tick = NULL

entries.expected_behavior: Synthetic players should exhibit realistic idle/offline transitions based on their archetype activity profile. 'Mostly Idle' players should go idle naturally; 'Hardcore' players should stay active.
entries.observed_behavior: FreshLifecycleRunner blanket-updates last_activity_tick for ALL players every tick (simulated_player_keepalive adapted path), preventing any player from ever going idle or offline. All players stay permanently Active regardless of archetype.
entries.notes: The keepalive is documented as an adapted path. Making it archetype-aware requires reading archetype activity profiles from the cohort manifest and selectively refreshing. Deferred to 5B or later.

entries.expected_behavior: presenceIsStale() should detect stale presence in simulation the same way it does in production.
entries.observed_behavior: presenceIsStale() uses time() (wall-clock) to compare against last_seen_at. In fast simulation, wall-clock barely advances so presenceIsStale() always returns false. Players can never transition to Offline via the presenceIsStale gate in simulation.
entries.notes: Production behavior is correct. The wall-clock dependency is the right metric for real HTTP traffic. In simulation, the keepalive (TMC-5A-002) already prevents idle transitions, so this is a secondary concern. A simulation-time-aware presenceIsStale adapter could be introduced if idle parity is needed.

entries.expected_behavior: Action eligibility gating (idle_modal_active) and economic presence resolution (resolveEconomicPresenceState) use the same logical state.
entries.observed_behavior: These are intentionally separate layers: idle_modal_active is a UI-level action gate (player can't act until they acknowledge the idle modal), while economic_presence_state is the tick-level economic gate (determines UBI rates and drop eligibility). A player who dismissed the modal but is still Idle economically can act but receives Idle-rate UBI. This is by design but creates a simulator parity challenge since the simulator bypasses the UI layer entirely.
entries.notes: Not a bug. Classified as parity_risk because the simulator cannot exercise the idle_modal UI flow.

entries.expected_behavior: resolveSigilDropActivityState and resolveEconomicPresenceState resolve consistently for all presence states.
entries.observed_behavior: When online_current=0 but activity_state='Active' (stale from before disconnect), drop resolution returns Offline (checks online_current first) while UBI resolution returns Active (checks activity_state first). In practice this state rarely occurs since TickEngine clears online_current and sets activity_state when it detects Offline. But the code paths have different resolution order.
entries.notes: Low-impact in practice because TickEngine synchronizes both fields. The divergence only matters in an edge case where online_current is cleared by a different code path than TickEngine (e.g., auth layer connection tracking). Test added to document this behavior: testOfflinePlayerConsistentAcrossPresenceResolvers.

entries.expected_behavior: Boost purchases should be dispatched through production Actions paths during simulation.
entries.observed_behavior: Boost purchases are not dispatched in FreshLifecycleRunner because the production boost activation path is tightly coupled to HTTP/session sigil spending UI calls. Already recorded as unmodeled mechanic 'boost_purchase_not_dispatched'.
entries.notes: TickEngine processes existing active_boosts faithfully; only boost creation is not exercised. Deferred to a future milestone when boost purchase can be adapted.

entries.expected_behavior: Self-melt freeze should be dispatched through production Actions::selfMeltFreeze during simulation.
entries.observed_behavior: PolicyBehavior does not implement a decision function for self-melt. No synthetic player will self-melt in the current milestone. Already recorded as unmodeled mechanic 'self_melt_freeze_no_policy_decision'.

entries.expected_behavior: rebalanceExistingSeasons should not silently rewrite economy config on seasons during simulation.
entries.observed_behavior: rebalanceExistingSeasons is correctly guarded — ensureSeasons() bails early when simulationTick is set, so rebalanceExistingSeasons never runs in simulation. In production, it runs every tick but is idempotent (WHERE clause matches only legacy-config seasons with base_ubi_active_per_tick=100 AND target_spend_rate_per_tick=50). No fix needed.
entries.notes: Verified correct: simulation guard is in place. Production behavior is idempotent migration logic.
